name: Charter Act 1853
summary: The last renewal of the Company's charter — this time without a fixed term, leaving Parliament free to revoke it at any time. It introduced competitive examinations for the Indian Civil Service (open to Indians in principle) and separated executive from legislative functions of the Governor-General's Council.
key_provisions: Charter renewed without a fixed term — Parliament may revoke at will
Competitive examinations for the Indian Civil Service — open to all subjects
Legislative Council separated from Executive Council; six additional Legislative members
Six additional Legislative Councillors representing the major provinces
Standing Parliamentary committee on Indian affairs established
demise: Mutiny of 1857 made renewal moot; Government of India Act 1858 abolished the Company.
